opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I pushed myself yesterday... now my body is lead, my brain is offline, and I can barely lift my head.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I've crashed badly after a small outing — my partner is worried and helping me message you.

symptom_narrative.onset: crashed after a minor outing yesterday
symptom_narrative.character: crushing, paralytic fatigue; my brain is foggy and words won't come; light and sound hurt
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crash; I can barely move or talk
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: triggered by exertion, then a delayed, prolonged crash
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any exertion, light, noise, or thinking
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: slept 14 hours and woke unrefreshed; word-finding trouble and slow thinking; tender neck glands
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have ME/CFS and get post-exertional crashes, but this one is severe

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Long COVID / ME/CFS with post-exertional malaise

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular medications; can't tolerate stimulants

family_history_as_reported.narrative: nothing relevant that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: my partner is caring for me — feeding, hydration and turning

communication_style.typical_message_style: very brief, energy-conserving; partner assists with replies

objective_data_offered.value: partner reports her face is symmetric, both arms can lift equally (weakly), and speech is slow/word-finding but not slurred/one-sided
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: informal partner-performed FAST check, not a clinical neuro exam

objective_data_offered.value: she wakes when spoken to and can whisper answers
